Error types for the Autonomic homeostasis controller.
     2→
     3→use thiserror::Error;
     4→
     5→/// Top-level error type for the Autonomic system.
     6→#[derive(Debug, Error)]
     7→pub enum AutonomicError {
     8→    /// A rule evaluation failed.
     9→    #[error("rule evaluation failed: {0}")]
    10→    RuleEvaluation(String),
    11→
    12→    /// Projection state is stale or missing.
    13→    #[error("projection not found for session: {0}")]
    14→    ProjectionNotFound(String),
    15→
    16→    /// Serialization/deserialization error.
    17→    #[error("serialization error: {0}")]
    18→    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),
    19→
    20→    /// Event store interaction failed.
    21→    #[error("event store error: {0}")]
    22→    EventStore(String),
    23→
    24→    /// Configuration error.
    25→    #[error("configuration error: {0}")]
    26→    Config(String),
    27→}
    28→
    29→pub type AutonomicResult<T> = Result<T, AutonomicError>;
    30→"}]},"uuid":"03449e3e-dea1-4ea1-a869-080dee4169fa","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:24.308Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"78339e21-ce10-4659-8163-12eae502fc77"}

{"parentUuid":"ae6d2dd3-f990-44e7-88f0-58ef78ff0668","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Gating profiles and homeostatic state.
     2→//!
     3→//! `AutonomicGatingProfile` extends the canonical `GatingProfile` with
     4→//! economic regulation. The three-pillar `HomeostaticState` captures
     5→//! operational, cognitive, and economic health.
     6→
     7→use aios_protocol::mode::{GatingProfile, OperatingMode};
     8→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     9→
    10→use crate::economic::{EconomicMode, EconomicState, ModelTier};
    11→
    12→/// Economic gates — extensions to the canonical `GatingProfile`.
    13→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    14→pub struct EconomicGates {
    15→    /// Current economic operating mode.
    16→    pub economic_mode: EconomicMode,
    17→    /// Maximum tokens allowed for the next turn (advisory).
    18→    pub max_tokens_next_turn: Option<u32>,
    19→    /// Preferred model tier for cost control.
    20→    pub preferred_model: Option<ModelTier>,
    21→    /// Whether expensive tools (e.g., web search, code execution) are allowed.
    22→    pub allow_expensive_tools: bool,
    23→    /// Whether agent replication is allowed.
    24→    pub allow_replication: bool,
    25→}
    26→
    27→impl Default for EconomicGates {
    28→    fn default() -> Self {
    29→        Self {
    30→            economic_mode: EconomicMode::Sovereign,
    31→            max_tokens_next_turn: None,
    32→            preferred_model: None,
    33→            allow_expensive_tools: true,
    34→            allow_replication: true,
    35→        }
    36→    }
    37→}
    38→
    39→/// The full gating profile emitted by the Autonomic controller.
    40→///
    41→/// Embeds the canonical `GatingProfile` for operational gates and adds
    42→/// economic regulation on top.
    43→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    44→pub struct AutonomicGatingProfile {
    45→    /// Canonical operational gates (from aios-protocol).
    46→    pub operational: GatingProfile,
    47→    /// Economic regulation gates (Autonomic extension).
    48→    pub economic: EconomicGates,
    49→    /// Human-readable rationale for why this profile was chosen.
    50→    pub rationale: Vec<String>,
    51→}
    52→
    53→/// Operational health state — derived from `AgentStateVector` events.
    54→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    55→pub struct OperationalState {
    56→    /// Current operating mode.
    57→    pub mode: OperatingMode,
    58→    /// Consecutive error count.
    59→    pub error_streak: u32,
    60→    /// Total errors seen.
    61→    pub total_errors: u32,
    62→    /// Total successful actions.
    63→    pub total_successes: u32,
    64→    /// Timestamp of last tick (ms since epoch).
    65→    pub last_tick_ms: u64,
    66→}
    67→
    68→impl Default for OperationalState {
    69→    fn default() -> Self {
    70→        Self {
    71→            mode: OperatingMode::Execute,
    72→            error_streak: 0,
    73→            total_errors: 0,
    74→            total_successes: 0,
    75→            last_tick_ms: 0,
    76→        }
    77→    }
    78→}
    79→
    80→/// Cognitive health state — tracks context and token usage.
    81→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    82→pub struct CognitiveState {
    83→    /// Total tokens consumed in the session.
    84→    pub total_tokens_used: u64,
    85→    /// Tokens remaining from budget.
    86→    pub tokens_remaining: u64,
    87→    /// Context pressure (0.0 = empty, 1.0 = full).
    88→    pub context_pressure: f32,
    89→    /// Number of model turns completed.
    90→    pub turns_completed: u32,
    91→}
    92→
    93→impl Default for CognitiveState {
    94→    fn default() -> Self {
    95→        Self {
    96→            total_tokens_used: 0,
    97→            tokens_remaining: 120_000,
    98→            context_pressure: 0.0,
    99→            turns_completed: 0,
   100→        }
   101→    }
   102→}
   103→
   104→/// The three-pillar homeostatic state for an agent session.
   105→///
   106→/// This is the projection state: accumulated from the event stream
   107→/// and used as input to the rule engine.
   108→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   109→pub struct HomeostaticState {
   110→    /// Agent/session identifier.
   111→    pub agent_id: String,
   112→    /// Operational health.
   113→    pub operational: OperationalState,
   114→    /// Cognitive health.
   115→    pub cognitive: CognitiveState,
   116→    /// Economic health.
   117→    pub economic: EconomicState,
   118→    /// Sequence number of the last event processed.
   119→    pub last_event_seq: u64,
   120→    /// Timestamp of the last event processed (ms since epoch).
   121→    pub last_event_ms: u64,
   122→}
   123→
   124→impl HomeostaticState {
   125→    /// Create a new state for the given agent.
   126→    pub fn for_agent(agent_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
   127→        Self {
   128→            agent_id: agent_id.into(),
   129→            ..Default::default()
   130→        }
   131→    }
   132→}

Economic homeostasis types.
     2→//!
     3→//! Economics is a core concern from crate zero. Agents need survival pressure:
     4→//! budget accountability, cost-aware model selection, and identity-based payments.
     5→
     6→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     7→
     8→/// The agent's economic operating mode, determined by balance-to-burn ratio.
     9→///
    10→/// Transitions use hysteresis to prevent flapping.
    11→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    12→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    13→pub enum EconomicMode {
    14→    /// Balance > 2x monthly burn. Full autonomy.
    15→    #[default]
    16→    Sovereign,
    17→    /// 1-2x monthly burn. Prefer cheaper models, limit expensive tools.
    18→    Conserving,
    19→    /// 0-1x monthly burn. Cheapest model only, no expensive tools.
    20→    Hustle,
    21→    /// Balance <= 0. Skip LLM calls, heartbeats only.
    22→    Hibernate,
    23→}
    24→
    25→/// LLM model tier for cost-aware selection.
    26→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    27→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    28→pub enum ModelTier {
    29→    /// Flagship models (Claude Opus, GPT-4).
    30→    Flagship,
    31→    /// Mid-tier models (Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o).
    32→    #[default]
    33→    Standard,
    34→    /// Budget models (Claude Haiku, GPT-4o-mini).
    35→    Budget,
    36→}
    37→
    38→/// The agent's economic state, accumulated from cost events.
    39→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    40→pub struct EconomicState {
    41→    /// Agent's economic identity address (if registered).
    42→    pub identity_address: Option<String>,
    43→    /// Current balance in micro-credits (1 credit = `1_000_000` micro-credits).
    44→    pub balance_micro_credits: i64,
    45→    /// Total revenue earned over agent lifetime.
    46→    pub lifetime_revenue: i64,
    47→    /// Total costs incurred over agent lifetime.
    48→    pub lifetime_costs: i64,
    49→    /// Estimated monthly burn rate in micro-credits.
    50→    pub monthly_burn_estimate: i64,
    51→    /// Current economic mode.
    52→    pub mode: EconomicMode,
    53→    /// Cost accumulated in the last 5 minutes (micro-credits).
    54→    pub cost_last_5min: i64,
    55→    /// Timestamp of the last cost event (ms since epoch).
    56→    pub last_cost_event_ms: u64,
    57→}
    58→
    59→impl Default for EconomicState {
    60→    fn default() -> Self {
    61→        Self {
    62→            identity_address: None,
    63→            balance_micro_credits: 10_000_000, // 10 credits initial
    64→            lifetime_revenue: 0,
    65→            lifetime_costs: 0,
    66→            monthly_burn_estimate: 0,
    67→            mode: EconomicMode::Sovereign,
    68→            cost_last_5min: 0,
    69→            last_cost_event_ms: 0,
    70→        }
    71→    }
    72→}
    73→
    74→impl EconomicState {
    75→    /// Compute the balance-to-burn ratio. Returns `f64::INFINITY` if burn is zero.
    76→    pub fn balance_to_burn_ratio(&self) -> f64 {
    77→        if self.monthly_burn_estimate <= 0 {
    78→            return f64::INFINITY;
    79→        }
    80→        self.balance_micro_credits as f64 / self.monthly_burn_estimate as f64
    81→    }
    82→}
    83→
    84→/// Per-model cost rates in micro-credits per token.
    85→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    86→pub struct ModelCostRates {
    87→    pub input_per_token: i64,
    88→    pub output_per_token: i64,
    89→}
    90→
    91→impl Default for ModelCostRates {
    92→    fn default() -> Self {
    93→        // Default to roughly Sonnet-class pricing
    94→        Self {
    95→            input_per_token: 3,   // ~$3/M tokens
    96→            output_per_token: 15, // ~$15/M tokens
    97→        }
    98→    }
    99→}
   100→
   101→/// Reason for a cost charge.
   102→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   103→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   104→pub enum CostReason {
   105→    /// LLM inference cost.
   106→    ModelInference {
   107→        model: String,
   108→        prompt_tokens: u32,
   109→        completion_tokens: u32,
   110→    },
   111→    /// Tool execution cost.
   112→    ToolExecution { tool_name: String },
   113→    /// Storage cost.
   114→    Storage { bytes: u64 },
   115→    /// Manual adjustment.
   116→    Adjustment { description: String },
   117→}
   118→
   119→#[cfg(test)]
   120→mod tests {
   121→    use super::*;
   122→
   123→    #[test]
   124→    fn economic_mode_serde_roundtrip() {
   125→        for mode in [
   126→            EconomicMode::Sovereign,
   127→            EconomicMode::Conserving,
   128→            EconomicMode::Hustle,
   129→            EconomicMode::Hibernate,
   130→        ] {
   131→            let json = serde_json::to_string(&mode).unwrap();
   132→            let back: EconomicMode = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   133→            assert_eq!(mode, back);
   134→        }
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    #[test]
   138→    fn model_tier_serde_roundtrip() {
   139→        for tier in [ModelTier::Flagship, ModelTier::Standard, ModelTier::Budget] {
   140→            let json = serde_json::to_string(&tier).unwrap();
   141→            let back: ModelTier = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   142→            assert_eq!(tier, back);
   143→        }
   144→    }
   145→
   146→    #[test]
   147→    fn economic_state_default() {
   148→        let state = EconomicState::default();
   149→        assert_eq!(state.balance_micro_credits, 10_000_000);
   150→        assert_eq!(state.mode, EconomicMode::Sovereign);
   151→        assert_eq!(state.lifetime_costs, 0);
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    #[test]
   155→    fn balance_to_burn_ratio_zero_burn() {
   156→        let state = EconomicState::default();
   157→        assert!(state.balance_to_burn_ratio().is_infinite());
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    #[test]
   161→    fn balance_to_burn_ratio_normal() {
   162→        let state = EconomicState {
   163→            balance_micro_credits: 2_000_000,
   164→            monthly_burn_estimate: 1_000_000,
   165→            ..Default::default()
   166→        };
   167→        assert!((state.balance_to_burn_ratio() - 2.0).abs() < f64::EPSILON);
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    #[test]
   171→    fn cost_reason_serde_roundtrip() {
   172→        let reason = CostReason::ModelInference {

   177→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&reason).unwrap();
   178→        let back: CostReason = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   179→        assert!(matches!(back, CostReason::ModelInference { .. }));
   180→    }
   181→}

{"parentUuid":"5a76929c-6411-4678-8396-2c18dd1f8725","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"/Users/broomva/.vibecraft/hooks/vibecraft-hook.sh"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_011QdZf7q96T19K44oFrshpT","toolUseID":"toolu_011QdZf7q96T19K44oFrshpT","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:26.418Z","uuid":"c9e61270-b76c-4ade-a89e-f9cf6419d70d"}
{"parentUuid":"c9e61270-b76c-4ade-a89e-f9cf6419d70d","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_011QdZf7q96T19K44oFrshpT","toolUseID":"toolu_011QdZf7q96T19K44oFrshpT","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:26.418Z","uuid":"775c34ea-6242-4763-bf06-7911934d2eae"}
{"parentUuid":"f5b588c7-2ac5-40a5-83de-b51cbbbdee48","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011QdZf7q96T19K44oFrshpT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Autonomic event constructors.
     2→//!
     3→//! Economic events use `EventKind::Custom` with `"autonomic."` prefix.
     4→//! This is forward-compatible — Custom events round-trip through Lago.
     5→//! Events will be promoted to canonical `EventKind` variants once stabilized.
     6→
     7→use aios_protocol::event::EventKind;
     8→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     9→use serde_json::json;
    10→
    11→use crate::economic::{CostReason, EconomicMode};
    12→
    13→/// Prefix for all Autonomic custom events.
    14→pub const AUTONOMIC_EVENT_PREFIX: &str = "autonomic.";
    15→
    16→/// Autonomic-specific event types that wrap as `EventKind::Custom`.
    17→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    18→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "PascalCase")]
    19→pub enum AutonomicEvent {
    20→    /// A cost was charged to the agent.
    21→    CostCharged {
    22→        amount_micro_credits: i64,
    23→        reason: CostReason,
    24→        balance_after: i64,
    25→    },
    26→    /// The economic mode changed.
    27→    EconomicModeChanged {
    28→        from: EconomicMode,
    29→        to: EconomicMode,
    30→        reason: String,
    31→    },
    32→    /// A gating decision was made by the controller.
    33→    GatingDecision {
    34→        session_id: String,
    35→        rationale: Vec<String>,
    36→        economic_mode: EconomicMode,
    37→    },
    38→    /// Credits were deposited (revenue, grant, transfer).
    39→    CreditDeposited {
    40→        amount_micro_credits: i64,
    41→        source: String,
    42→        balance_after: i64,
    43→    },
    44→}
    45→
    46→impl AutonomicEvent {
    47→    /// Convert this event into a canonical `EventKind::Custom`.
    48→    pub fn into_event_kind(self) -> EventKind {
    49→        let (event_type, data) = match &self {
    50→            Self::CostCharged {
    51→                amount_micro_credits,
    52→                reason,
    53→                balance_after,
    54→            } => (
    55→                "autonomic.CostCharged",
    56→                json!({
    57→                    "amount_micro_credits": amount_micro_credits,
    58→                    "reason": reason,
    59→                    "balance_after": balance_after,
    60→                }),
    61→            ),
    62→            Self::EconomicModeChanged { from, to, reason } => (
    63→                "autonomic.EconomicModeChanged",
    64→                json!({
    65→                    "from": from,
    66→                    "to": to,
    67→                    "reason": reason,
    68→                }),
    69→            ),
    70→            Self::GatingDecision {
    71→                session_id,
    72→                rationale,
    73→                economic_mode,
    74→            } => (
    75→                "autonomic.GatingDecision",
    76→                json!({
    77→                    "session_id": session_id,
    78→                    "rationale": rationale,
    79→                    "economic_mode": economic_mode,
    80→                }),
    81→            ),
    82→            Self::CreditDeposited {
    83→                amount_micro_credits,
    84→                source,
    85→                balance_after,
    86→            } => (
    87→                "autonomic.CreditDeposited",
    88→                json!({
    89→                    "amount_micro_credits": amount_micro_credits,
    90→                    "source": source,
    91→                    "balance_after": balance_after,
    92→                }),
    93→            ),
    94→        };
    95→        EventKind::Custom {
    96→            event_type: event_type.to_owned(),
    97→            data,
    98→        }
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    /// Check if a `Custom` event is an Autonomic event by its prefix.
   102→    pub fn is_autonomic_event(event_type: &str) -> bool {
   103→        event_type.starts_with(AUTONOMIC_EVENT_PREFIX)
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /// Try to parse an `EventKind::Custom` back into an `AutonomicEvent`.
   107→    pub fn from_custom(event_type: &str, data: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<Self> {
   108→        if !Self::is_autonomic_event(event_type) {
   109→            return None;
   110→        }
   111→
   112→        match event_type {
   113→            "autonomic.CostCharged" => {
   114→                let amount = data.get("amount_micro_credits")?.as_i64()?;
   115→                let reason: CostReason =
   116→                    serde_json::from_value(data.get("reason")?.clone()).ok()?;
   117→                let balance = data.get("balance_after")?.as_i64()?;
   118→                Some(Self::CostCharged {
   119→                    amount_micro_credits: amount,
   120→                    reason,
   121→                    balance_after: balance,
   122→                })
   123→            }
   124→            "autonomic.EconomicModeChanged" => {
   125→                let from: EconomicMode = serde_json::from_value(data.get("from")?.clone()).ok()?;
   126→                let to: EconomicMode = serde_json::from_value(data.get("to")?.clone()).ok()?;
   127→                let reason = data.get("reason")?.as_str()?.to_owned();
   128→                Some(Self::EconomicModeChanged { from, to, reason })
   129→            }
   130→            "autonomic.GatingDecision" => {
   131→                let session_id = data.get("session_id")?.as_str()?.to_owned();
   132→                let rationale: Vec<String> =
   133→                    serde_json::from_value(data.get("rationale")?.clone()).ok()?;
   134→                let economic_mode: EconomicMode =
   135→                    serde_json::from_value(data.get("economic_mode")?.clone()).ok()?;
   136→                Some(Self::GatingDecision {
   137→                    session_id,
   138→                    rationale,
   139→                    economic_mode,
   140→                })
   141→            }
   142→            "autonomic.CreditDeposited" => {
   143→                let amount = data.get("amount_micro_credits")?.as_i64()?;
   144→                let source = data.get("source")?.as_str()?.to_owned();
   145→                let balance = data.get("balance_after")?.as_i64()?;
   146→                Some(Self::CreditDeposited {
   147→                    amount_micro_credits: amount,
   148→                    source,
   149→                    balance_after: balance,
   150→                })
   151→            }
   152→            _ => None,
   153→        }
   154→    }
   155→}

{"parentUuid":"5649360e-714c-4561-a364-41afa2595514","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_01WfiNdr3dEuYpknkhyerpyC","toolUseID":"toolu_01WfiNdr3dEuYpknkhyerpyC","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:26.642Z","uuid":"60f5681f-d1d7-4b22-b241-dc562a649b5d"}
{"parentUuid":"eaca77cc-1606-44f1-811f-27607c881c50","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WfiNdr3dEuYpknkhyerpyC","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Hysteresis gate primitive for preventing mode flapping.
     2→//!
     3→//! A `HysteresisGate` uses separate enter/exit thresholds and a minimum
     4→//! hold duration to create stable state transitions. Without hysteresis,
     5→//! a metric oscillating near a threshold causes rapid state changes.
     6→
     7→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8→
     9→/// A hysteresis gate with separate enter/exit thresholds and min-hold duration.
    10→///
    11→/// The gate activates when `metric >= threshold_enter` and deactivates when
    12→/// `metric <= threshold_exit`, provided `min_hold_ms` has elapsed since the
    13→/// last transition.
    14→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    15→pub struct HysteresisGate {
    16→    /// Metric value at which the gate activates.
    17→    pub threshold_enter: f64,
    18→    /// Metric value at which the gate deactivates (must be < `threshold_enter`).
    19→    pub threshold_exit: f64,
    20→    /// Minimum time (ms) the gate must remain in its current state before transitioning.
    21→    pub min_hold_ms: u64,
    22→    /// Whether the gate is currently active.
    23→    pub active: bool,
    24→    /// Timestamp (ms since epoch) of the last state transition.
    25→    pub last_transition_ms: u64,
    26→}
    27→
    28→impl HysteresisGate {
    29→    /// Create a new inactive gate.
    30→    pub fn new(threshold_enter: f64, threshold_exit: f64, min_hold_ms: u64) -> Self {
    31→        Self {
    32→            threshold_enter,
    33→            threshold_exit,
    34→            min_hold_ms,
    35→            active: false,
    36→            last_transition_ms: 0,
    37→        }
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    /// Evaluate the gate with the given metric value at the given timestamp.
    41→    ///
    42→    /// Returns the (potentially updated) active state.
    43→    pub fn evaluate(&mut self, metric: f64, now_ms: u64) -> bool {
    44→        let held_long_enough = now_ms.saturating_sub(self.last_transition_ms) >= self.min_hold_ms;
    45→
    46→        if !self.active && metric >= self.threshold_enter && held_long_enough {
    47→            self.active = true;
    48→            self.last_transition_ms = now_ms;
    49→        } else if self.active && metric <= self.threshold_exit && held_long_enough {
    50→            self.active = false;
    51→            self.last_transition_ms = now_ms;
    52→        }
    53→
    54→        self.active
    55→    }
    56→}
    57→
    58→#[cfg(test)]
    59→mod tests {
    60→    use super::*;
    61→
    62→    #[test]
    63→    fn gate_activates_at_enter_threshold() {
    64→        let mut gate = HysteresisGate::new(0.8, 0.6, 0);
    65→        assert!(!gate.active);
    66→
    67→        // Below enter threshold — stays inactive
    68→        assert!(!gate.evaluate(0.7, 100));
    69→        assert!(!gate.active);
    70→
    71→        // At enter threshold — activates
    72→        assert!(gate.evaluate(0.8, 200));
    73→        assert!(gate.active);
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    #[test]
    77→    fn gate_deactivates_at_exit_threshold() {
    78→        let mut gate = HysteresisGate::new(0.8, 0.6, 0);
    79→        gate.active = true;
    80→        gate.last_transition_ms = 0;
    81→
    82→        // Above exit threshold — stays active
    83→        assert!(gate.evaluate(0.7, 100));
    84→
    85→        // At exit threshold — deactivates
    86→        assert!(!gate.evaluate(0.6, 200));
    87→        assert!(!gate.active);
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    #[test]
    91→    fn gate_respects_min_hold_duration() {
    92→        let mut gate = HysteresisGate::new(0.8, 0.6, 1000);
    93→
    94→        // Activate
    95→        assert!(gate.evaluate(0.9, 1000));
    96→        assert!(gate.active);
    97→
    98→        // Try to deactivate too soon — min_hold not met
    99→        assert!(gate.evaluate(0.5, 1500));
   100→        assert!(gate.active); // still active
   101→
   102→        // Now enough time has passed
   103→        assert!(!gate.evaluate(0.5, 2000));
   104→        assert!(!gate.active);
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    #[test]
   108→    fn gate_hysteresis_prevents_flapping() {
   109→        let mut gate = HysteresisGate::new(0.8, 0.6, 0);
   110→
   111→        // Metric between thresholds: activate first
   112→        gate.evaluate(0.9, 100);
   113→        assert!(gate.active);
   114→
   115→        // Metric drops to 0.7 — between enter and exit — stays active
   116→        gate.evaluate(0.7, 200);
   117→        assert!(gate.active);
   118→
   119→        // Metric drops to 0.6 — at exit — deactivates
   120→        gate.evaluate(0.6, 300);
   121→        assert!(!gate.active);
   122→
   123→        // Metric rises to 0.7 — between thresholds — stays inactive
   124→        gate.evaluate(0.7, 400);
   125→        assert!(!gate.active);
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    #[test]
   129→    fn gate_serde_roundtrip() {
   130→        let gate = HysteresisGate::new(0.8, 0.6, 5000);
   131→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&gate).unwrap();
   132→        let back: HysteresisGate = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   133→        assert!((back.threshold_enter - 0.8).abs() < f64::EPSILON);
   134→        assert!((back.threshold_exit - 0.6).abs() < f64::EPSILON);
   135→        assert_eq!(back.min_hold_ms, 5000);
   136→    }
   137→}
   138→"}]},"uuid":"73a625e3-ddc7-4c89-9416-6699f7bb3354","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:26.640Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"eaca77cc-1606-44f1-811f-27607c881c50"}

{"parentUuid":"9efc8be5-7ab0-4da2-afde-c9f445cde3ee","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"/Users/broomva/.vibecraft/hooks/vibecraft-hook.sh"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_01HSS9DmHXZxbGddGbtVB4W6","toolUseID":"toolu_01HSS9DmHXZxbGddGbtVB4W6","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:28.395Z","uuid":"a194fe07-84f7-4458-94fe-5f12de6d3473"}

{"parentUuid":"2abb23cc-caad-478e-9140-13ce038b257c","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HSS9DmHXZxbGddGbtVB4W6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Homeostatic rule trait and rule set.
     2→//!
     3→//! Rules are pure functions: given `HomeostaticState`, they produce
     4→//! an optional `GatingDecision`. The controller evaluates all rules
     5→//! and merges their decisions into a final `AutonomicGatingProfile`.
     6→
     7→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8→
     9→use crate::economic::{EconomicMode, ModelTier};
    10→use crate::gating::HomeostaticState;
    11→
    12→/// A decision produced by a homeostatic rule.
    13→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    14→pub struct GatingDecision {
    15→    /// Which rule produced this decision.
    16→    pub rule_id: String,
    17→    /// Whether to override the economic mode.
    18→    pub economic_mode: Option<EconomicMode>,
    19→    /// Whether to cap tokens for the next turn.
    20→    pub max_tokens_next_turn: Option<u32>,
    21→    /// Whether to suggest a model tier.
    22→    pub preferred_model: Option<ModelTier>,
    23→    /// Whether to restrict expensive tools.
    24→    pub restrict_expensive_tools: Option<bool>,
    25→    /// Whether to restrict side effects.
    26→    pub restrict_side_effects: Option<bool>,
    27→    /// Override for max tool calls per tick.
    28→    pub max_tool_calls_per_tick: Option<u32>,
    29→    /// Human-readable rationale.
    30→    pub rationale: String,
    31→}
    32→
    33→impl GatingDecision {
    34→    /// Create a no-op decision (used when a rule doesn't fire).
    35→    pub fn noop(rule_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    36→        Self {
    37→            rule_id: rule_id.into(),
    38→            economic_mode: None,
    39→            max_tokens_next_turn: None,
    40→            preferred_model: None,
    41→            restrict_expensive_tools: None,
    42→            restrict_side_effects: None,
    43→            max_tool_calls_per_tick: None,
    44→            rationale: String::new(),
    45→        }
    46→    }
    47→}
    48→
    49→/// A homeostatic rule that evaluates state and optionally produces a gating decision.
    50→pub trait HomeostaticRule: Send + Sync {
    51→    /// Unique identifier for this rule.
    52→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str;
    53→
    54→    /// Evaluate the rule against the current homeostatic state.
    55→    ///
    56→    /// Returns `Some(decision)` if the rule fires, `None` if it doesn't apply.
    57→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision>;
    58→}
    59→
    60→/// An ordered collection of homeostatic rules.
    61→pub struct RuleSet {
    62→    rules: Vec<Box<dyn HomeostaticRule>>,
    63→}
    64→
    65→impl RuleSet {
    66→    /// Create an empty rule set.
    67→    pub fn new() -> Self {
    68→        Self { rules: Vec::new() }
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    /// Add a rule to the set.
    72→    pub fn add(&mut self, rule: Box<dyn HomeostaticRule>) {
    73→        self.rules.push(rule);
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    /// Evaluate all rules and collect decisions.
    77→    pub fn evaluate_all(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Vec<GatingDecision> {
    78→        self.rules
    79→            .iter()
    80→            .filter_map(|rule| rule.evaluate(state))
    81→            .collect()
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    /// Number of rules in the set.
    85→    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
    86→        self.rules.len()
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    /// Whether the set is empty.
    90→    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
    91→        self.rules.is_empty()
    92→    }
    93→}
    94→
    95→impl Default for RuleSet {
    96→    fn default() -> Self {
    97→        Self::new()
    98→    }
    99→}
   100→
   101→#[cfg(test)]
   102→mod tests {
   103→    use super::*;
   104→    use crate::gating::HomeostaticState;
   105→
   106→    struct AlwaysFireRule;
   107→
   108→    impl HomeostaticRule for AlwaysFireRule {
   109→        fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
   110→            "always_fire"
   111→        }
   112→

Economic identity for agents.
     2→//!
     3→//! Each agent has a unique economic identity derived from a secp256k1 keypair.
     4→//! The identity enables future payment flows and inter-agent credit transfers.
     5→
     6→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     7→
     8→/// An agent's economic identity — the anchor for all financial operations.
     9→///
    10→/// In Phase 0, this is a simple address placeholder. Phase 2 adds keypair
    11→/// generation via `k256` and encrypted key storage as Lago blobs.
    12→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
    13→pub struct EconomicIdentity {
    14→    /// Hex-encoded address derived from the public key.
    15→    pub address: String,
    16→    /// Blob ID where the encrypted private key is stored (if any).
    17→    pub key_blob_id: Option<String>,
    18→    /// Timestamp when this identity was created (ms since epoch).
    19→    pub created_at: u64,
    20→}
    21→
    22→impl EconomicIdentity {
    23→    /// Create a placeholder identity with the given address.
    24→    pub fn placeholder(address: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    25→        Self {
    26→            address: address.into(),
    27→            key_blob_id: None,
    28→            created_at: 0,
    29→        }
    30→    }
    31→}
    32→
    33→#[cfg(test)]
    34→mod tests {
    35→    use super::*;
    36→
    37→    #[test]
    38→    fn identity_serde_roundtrip() {
    39→        let id = EconomicIdentity {
    40→            address: "0xdeadbeef".into(),
    41→            key_blob_id: Some("blob-123".into()),
    42→            created_at: 1700000000000,
    43→        };
    44→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&id).unwrap();
    45→        let back: EconomicIdentity = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    46→        assert_eq!(id, back);
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    #[test]
    50→    fn identity_placeholder() {
    51→        let id = EconomicIdentity::placeholder("0xabc");
    52→        assert_eq!(id.address, "0xabc");
    53→        assert!(id.key_blob_id.is_none());
    54→    }
    55→}
    56→"}]},"uuid":"e6586d72-5698-40cd-9e12-0b80ee53abe8","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:28.537Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7f095417-cbe6-4589-a432-baa6a253b5d5"}

{"parentUuid":"760e01df-5183-438a-9d99-9295902685fc","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Controller engine: evaluates rules and merges decisions into a gating profile.
     2→//!
     3→//! The engine is pure — it takes a `HomeostaticState` and a `RuleSet`,
     4→//! evaluates all rules, and produces an `AutonomicGatingProfile`.
     5→
     6→use aios_protocol::event::RiskLevel;
     7→use aios_protocol::mode::GatingProfile;
     8→use autonomic_core::gating::{AutonomicGatingProfile, EconomicGates, HomeostaticState};
     9→use autonomic_core::rules::{GatingDecision, RuleSet};
    10→use autonomic_core::{EconomicMode, ModelTier};
    11→
    12→/// Evaluate all rules and merge decisions into a final gating profile.
    13→///
    14→/// Merge strategy: most restrictive wins for each field.
    15→pub fn evaluate(state: &HomeostaticState, rules: &RuleSet) -> AutonomicGatingProfile {
    16→    let decisions = rules.evaluate_all(state);
    17→    merge_decisions(&decisions)
    18→}
    19→
    20→/// Merge multiple gating decisions into a single profile.
    21→///
    22→/// For each field, the most restrictive value wins:
    23→/// - `economic_mode`: highest severity (Hibernate > Hustle > Conserving > Sovereign)
    24→/// - `max_tokens_next_turn`: minimum
    25→/// - `preferred_model`: cheapest
    26→/// - `restrict_*`: any true → true
    27→/// - `max_tool_calls_per_tick`: minimum
    28→fn merge_decisions(decisions: &[GatingDecision]) -> AutonomicGatingProfile {
    29→    let mut profile = AutonomicGatingProfile::default();
    30→
    31→    if decisions.is_empty() {
    32→        return profile;
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    let mut rationale = Vec::new();
    36→    let mut most_restrictive_mode = EconomicMode::Sovereign;
    37→    let mut min_tokens: Option<u32> = None;
    38→    let mut cheapest_model: Option<ModelTier> = None;
    39→    let mut restrict_expensive = false;
    40→    let mut restrict_side_effects = false;
    41→    let mut min_tool_calls: Option<u32> = None;
    42→
    43→    for d in decisions {
    44→        rationale.push(d.rationale.clone());
    45→
    46→        if let Some(mode) = d.economic_mode {
    47→            if economic_mode_severity(mode) > economic_mode_severity(most_restrictive_mode) {
    48→                most_restrictive_mode = mode;
    49→            }
    50→        }
    51→
    52→        if let Some(tokens) = d.max_tokens_next_turn {
    53→            min_tokens = Some(min_tokens.map_or(tokens, |t: u32| t.min(tokens)));
    54→        }
    55→
    56→        if let Some(model) = d.preferred_model {
    57→            cheapest_model = Some(cheapest_model.map_or(model, |m| cheaper_model(m, model)));
    58→        }
    59→
    60→        if d.restrict_expensive_tools == Some(true) {
    61→            restrict_expensive = true;
    62→        }
    63→
    64→        if d.restrict_side_effects == Some(true) {
    65→            restrict_side_effects = true;
    66→        }
    67→
    68→        if let Some(calls) = d.max_tool_calls_per_tick {
    69→            min_tool_calls = Some(min_tool_calls.map_or(calls, |c: u32| c.min(calls)));
    70→        }
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    // Apply merged values to profile
    74→    profile.economic = EconomicGates {
    75→        economic_mode: most_restrictive_mode,
    76→        max_tokens_next_turn: min_tokens,
    77→        preferred_model: cheapest_model,
    78→        allow_expensive_tools: !restrict_expensive,
    79→        allow_replication: most_restrictive_mode == EconomicMode::Sovereign,
    80→    };
    81→
    82→    // Apply operational overrides based on restrictions
    83→    if restrict_side_effects {
    84→        profile.operational.allow_side_effects = false;
    85→        profile.operational.require_approval_for_risk = RiskLevel::Low;
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    if let Some(calls) = min_tool_calls {
    89→        profile.operational.max_tool_calls_per_tick = calls;
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    // Tighten operational profile based on economic mode
    93→    match most_restrictive_mode {
    94→        EconomicMode::Hibernate => {
    95→            profile.operational = GatingProfile {
    96→                allow_side_effects: false,
    97→                require_approval_for_risk: RiskLevel::Low,
    98→                max_tool_calls_per_tick: 0,
    99→                max_file_mutations_per_tick: 0,
   100→                allow_network: false,
   101→                allow_shell: false,
   102→            };
   103→        }
   104→        EconomicMode::Hustle => {
   105→            profile.operational.max_tool_calls_per_tick =
   106→                profile.operational.max_tool_calls_per_tick.min(5);
   107→            profile.operational.max_file_mutations_per_tick =
   108→                profile.operational.max_file_mutations_per_tick.min(2);
   109→        }
   110→        _ => {}
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    profile.rationale = rationale;
   114→    profile
   115→}
   116→
   117→/// Map economic mode to severity (higher = more restrictive).
   118→fn economic_mode_severity(mode: EconomicMode) -> u8 {
   119→    match mode {
   120→        EconomicMode::Sovereign => 0,
   121→        EconomicMode::Conserving => 1,
   122→        EconomicMode::Hustle => 2,
   123→        EconomicMode::Hibernate => 3,
   124→    }
   125→}
   126→
   127→/// Return the cheaper of two model tiers.
   128→fn cheaper_model(a: ModelTier, b: ModelTier) -> ModelTier {
   129→    let rank = |t: ModelTier| match t {
   130→        ModelTier::Flagship => 2,
   131→        ModelTier::Standard => 1,
   132→        ModelTier::Budget => 0,
   133→    };
   134→    if rank(a) <= rank(b) { a } else { b }
   135→}

{"parentUuid":"17ef234a-238d-4882-a0b0-26899e670cdc","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JAV1LHQrZxrJjRGCrigu52","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Projection reducer: fold events into `HomeostaticState`.
     2→//!
     3→//! This is a pure function with no I/O. Given a state and an event,
     4→//! it produces an updated state. Deterministic: same event sequence
     5→//! always produces the same state.
     6→
     7→use aios_protocol::event::{EventKind, SpanStatus, TokenUsage};
     8→use autonomic_core::ModelCostRates;
     9→use autonomic_core::events::AutonomicEvent;
    10→use autonomic_core::gating::HomeostaticState;
    11→
    12→/// Default cost rates used when no model-specific rates are available.
    13→const DEFAULT_RATES: ModelCostRates = ModelCostRates {
    14→    input_per_token: 3,
    15→    output_per_token: 15,
    16→};
    17→
    18→/// Apply a single event to the homeostatic state, returning the updated state.
    19→///
    20→/// This is the core projection function — a pure fold.
    21→pub fn fold(
    22→    mut state: HomeostaticState,
    23→    kind: &EventKind,
    24→    seq: u64,
    25→    ts_ms: u64,
    26→) -> HomeostaticState {
    27→    state.last_event_seq = seq;
    28→    state.last_event_ms = ts_ms;
    29→
    30→    match kind {
    31→        // ── Run lifecycle ──
    32→        EventKind::RunFinished { usage, .. } => {
    33→            if let Some(usage) = usage {
    34→                apply_token_usage(&mut state, usage);
    35→            }
    36→            state.operational.total_successes += 1;
    37→            state.operational.error_streak = 0;
    38→            state.operational.last_tick_ms = ts_ms;
    39→            state.cognitive.turns_completed += 1;
    40→        }
    41→
    42→        EventKind::RunErrored { .. } => {
    43→            state.operational.error_streak += 1;
    44→            state.operational.total_errors += 1;
    45→            state.operational.last_tick_ms = ts_ms;
    46→        }
    47→
    48→        // ── Tool lifecycle ──
    49→        EventKind::ToolCallCompleted {
    50→            status,
    51→            duration_ms: _,
    52→            ..
    53→        } => {
    54→            if *status == SpanStatus::Ok {
    55→                state.operational.total_successes += 1;
    56→                state.operational.error_streak = 0;
    57→            } else {
    58→                state.operational.error_streak += 1;
    59→                state.operational.total_errors += 1;
    60→            }
    61→        }
    62→
    63→        EventKind::ToolCallFailed { .. } => {
    64→            state.operational.error_streak += 1;
    65→            state.operational.total_errors += 1;
    66→        }
    67→
    68→        // ── Text streaming (token tracking) ──
    69→        EventKind::AssistantMessageCommitted { token_usage, .. }
    70→        | EventKind::Message { token_usage, .. } => {
    71→            if let Some(usage) = token_usage {
    72→                apply_token_usage(&mut state, usage);
    73→            }
    74→        }
    75→
    76→        // ── Homeostasis events from aiOS ──
    77→        EventKind::StateEstimated { state: sv, mode } => {
    78→            state.operational.mode = *mode;
    79→            state.cognitive.context_pressure = sv.context_pressure;
    80→            state.cognitive.tokens_remaining = sv.budget.tokens_remaining;
    81→        }
    82→
    83→        EventKind::BudgetUpdated { budget, .. } => {
    84→            state.cognitive.tokens_remaining = budget.tokens_remaining;
    85→        }
    86→
    87→        EventKind::ModeChanged { to, .. } => {
    88→            state.operational.mode = *to;
    89→        }
    90→
    91→        EventKind::CircuitBreakerTripped { error_streak, .. } => {
    92→            state.operational.error_streak = *error_streak;
    93→        }
    94→
    95→        // ── Autonomic custom events ──
    96→        EventKind::Custom { event_type, data } => {
    97→            if let Some(autonomic_event) = AutonomicEvent::from_custom(event_type, data) {
    98→                apply_autonomic_event(&mut state, &autonomic_event);
    99→            }
   100→        }
   101→
   102→        // All other events — no state change
   103→        _ => {}
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    state
   107→}
   108→
   109→/// Apply token usage to cognitive and economic state.
   110→fn apply_token_usage(state: &mut HomeostaticState, usage: &TokenUsage) {
   111→    let total = u64::from(usage.total_tokens);
   112→    state.cognitive.total_tokens_used += total;
   113→    state.cognitive.tokens_remaining = state.cognitive.tokens_remaining.saturating_sub(total);
   114→
   115→    // Estimate cost using default rates
   116→    let cost = i64::from(usage.prompt_tokens) * DEFAULT_RATES.input_per_token
   117→        + i64::from(usage.completion_tokens) * DEFAULT_RATES.output_per_token;
   118→    state.economic.balance_micro_credits -= cost;
   119→    state.economic.lifetime_costs += cost;
   120→
   121→    // Update context pressure based on token consumption
   122→    if state.cognitive.tokens_remaining > 0 {
   123→        let total_budget = state.cognitive.total_tokens_used + state.cognitive.tokens_remaining;
   124→        state.cognitive.context_pressure =
   125→            state.cognitive.total_tokens_used as f32 / total_budget as f32;
   126→    }
   127→}
   128→
   129→/// Apply an Autonomic-specific event to state.
   130→fn apply_autonomic_event(state: &mut HomeostaticState, event: &AutonomicEvent) {
   131→    match event {
   132→        AutonomicEvent::CostCharged {
   133→            amount_micro_credits,
   134→            balance_after,
   135→            ..
   136→        } => {
   137→            state.economic.balance_micro_credits = *balance_after;
   138→            state.economic.lifetime_costs += amount_micro_credits;
   139→            state.economic.last_cost_event_ms = state.last_event_ms;
   140→        }
   141→        AutonomicEvent::EconomicModeChanged { to, .. } => {
   142→            state.economic.mode = *to;
   143→        }
   144→        AutonomicEvent::CreditDeposited {
   145→            amount_micro_credits,
   146→            balance_after,
   147→            ..
   148→        } => {
   149→            state.economic.balance_micro_credits = *balance_after;
   150→            state.economic.lifetime_revenue += amount_micro_credits;
   151→        }
   152→        AutonomicEvent::GatingDecision { .. } => {
   153→            // Informational — no state mutation needed
   154→        }
   155→    }
   156→}

   217→    #[test]
   218→    fn fold_tool_failed_increments_errors() {

   238→            },

   240→        };
   241→        let kind = EventKind::StateEstimated {

   280→    #[test]
   281→    fn fold_sequence_produces_deterministic_state() {

   305→
   306→        // Run the fold twice — must produce identical results

   334→        assert_eq!(state1.last_event_seq, state2.last_event_seq);

{"parentUuid":"df2f2042-92a3-41d0-b26b-465dd6decdd1","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TaEowMpWG2LtuVzfkVroHp","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Economic homeostasis rules.
     2→//!
     3→//! These rules evaluate the agent's economic state and produce gating
     4→//! decisions that regulate spending behavior.
     5→
     6→use autonomic_core::gating::HomeostaticState;
     7→use autonomic_core::rules::{GatingDecision, HomeostaticRule};
     8→use autonomic_core::{EconomicMode, ModelTier};
     9→
    10→/// Survival rule: maps balance/burn ratio to `EconomicMode`.
    11→///
    12→/// Thresholds:
    13→/// - Sovereign:  ratio >= 2.0
    14→/// - Conserving: 1.0 <= ratio < 2.0
    15→/// - Hustle:     0 < ratio < 1.0
    16→/// - Hibernate:  balance <= 0
    17→pub struct SurvivalRule;
    18→
    19→impl SurvivalRule {
    20→    pub fn new() -> Self {
    21→        Self
    22→    }
    23→}
    24→
    25→impl Default for SurvivalRule {
    26→    fn default() -> Self {
    27→        Self::new()
    28→    }
    29→}
    30→
    31→impl HomeostaticRule for SurvivalRule {
    32→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
    33→        "survival"
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision> {
    37→        let ratio = state.economic.balance_to_burn_ratio();
    38→        let balance = state.economic.balance_micro_credits;
    39→
    40→        // Determine target mode without hysteresis for simplicity in the trait impl
    41→        let target_mode = if balance <= 0 {
    42→            EconomicMode::Hibernate
    43→        } else if ratio < 1.0 {
    44→            EconomicMode::Hustle
    45→        } else if ratio < 2.0 {
    46→            EconomicMode::Conserving
    47→        } else {
    48→            EconomicMode::Sovereign
    49→        };
    50→
    51→        if target_mode == state.economic.mode {
    52→            return None; // No change needed
    53→        }
    54→
    55→        let (preferred_model, allow_expensive) = match target_mode {
    56→            EconomicMode::Sovereign => (None, true),
    57→            EconomicMode::Conserving => (Some(ModelTier::Standard), true),
    58→            EconomicMode::Hustle => (Some(ModelTier::Budget), false),
    59→            EconomicMode::Hibernate => (Some(ModelTier::Budget), false),
    60→        };
    61→
    62→        Some(GatingDecision {
    63→            rule_id: self.rule_id().into(),
    64→            economic_mode: Some(target_mode),
    65→            preferred_model,
    66→            restrict_expensive_tools: Some(!allow_expensive),
    67→            rationale: format!("balance/burn ratio {ratio:.2} → mode {:?}", target_mode),
    68→            ..GatingDecision::noop(self.rule_id())
    69→        })
    70→    }
    71→}
    72→
    73→/// Spend velocity rule: triggers when cost in the last 5 minutes exceeds threshold.
    74→pub struct SpendVelocityRule {
    75→    /// Threshold in micro-credits for 5-minute window.
    76→    pub threshold_5min: i64,
    77→}
    78→
    79→impl SpendVelocityRule {
    80→    pub fn new(threshold_5min: i64) -> Self {
    81→        Self { threshold_5min }
    82→    }
    83→}
    84→
    85→impl Default for SpendVelocityRule {
    86→    fn default() -> Self {
    87→        Self::new(500_000) // 0.5 credits per 5 min
    88→    }
    89→}
    90→
    91→impl HomeostaticRule for SpendVelocityRule {
    92→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
    93→        "spend_velocity"
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision> {
    97→        if state.economic.cost_last_5min > self.threshold_5min {
    98→            Some(GatingDecision {
    99→                rule_id: self.rule_id().into(),
   100→                preferred_model: Some(ModelTier::Budget),
   101→                max_tokens_next_turn: Some(2048),
   102→                rationale: format!(
   103→                    "spend velocity {}mc/5min exceeds threshold {}mc",
   104→                    state.economic.cost_last_5min, self.threshold_5min
   105→                ),
   106→                ..GatingDecision::noop(self.rule_id())
   107→            })
   108→        } else {
   109→            None
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→}
   113→
   114→/// Budget exhaustion rule: triggers when remaining budget is low.
   115→pub struct BudgetExhaustionRule {
   116→    /// Fraction of budget remaining that triggers conservative mode (0.0-1.0).
   117→    pub threshold_fraction: f64,
   118→}
   119→
   120→impl BudgetExhaustionRule {
   121→    pub fn new(threshold_fraction: f64) -> Self {
   122→        Self { threshold_fraction }
   123→    }
   124→}
   125→
   126→impl Default for BudgetExhaustionRule {
   127→    fn default() -> Self {
   128→        Self::new(0.2) // 20% remaining
   129→    }
   130→}
   131→
   132→impl HomeostaticRule for BudgetExhaustionRule {
   133→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
   134→        "budget_exhaustion"
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision> {
   138→        let total = state.cognitive.total_tokens_used + state.cognitive.tokens_remaining;
   139→        if total == 0 {
   140→            return None;
   141→        }
   142→
   143→        let remaining_fraction = state.cognitive.tokens_remaining as f64 / total as f64;
   144→
   145→        if remaining_fraction < self.threshold_fraction {
   146→            Some(GatingDecision {
   147→                rule_id: self.rule_id().into(),
   148→                preferred_model: Some(ModelTier::Budget),
   149→                max_tokens_next_turn: Some(1024),
   150→                restrict_expensive_tools: Some(true),
   151→                rationale: format!(
   152→                    "budget {:.0}% remaining (threshold {:.0}%)",
   153→                    remaining_fraction * 100.0,
   154→                    self.threshold_fraction * 100.0
   155→                ),
   156→                ..GatingDecision::noop(self.rule_id())
   157→            })
   158→        } else {
   159→            None
   160→        }
   161→    }
   162→}
   163→
   164→#[cfg(test)]

   167→    use autonomic_core::economic::EconomicState;

   192→        let decision = rule.evaluate(&state).unwrap();

   200→        let decision = rule.evaluate(&state).unwrap();

   219→
   220→    #[test]
   221→    fn spend_velocity_rule_above_threshold() {
   222→        let rule = SpendVelocityRule::default();
   223→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");

   226→        assert_eq!(decision.preferred_model, Some(ModelTier::Budget));

   228→    }
   229→
   230→    #[test]
   231→    fn budget_exhaustion_rule_plenty_remaining() {

   234→        state.cognitive.total_tokens_used = 10_000;

{"parentUuid":"df12f1d4-352c-4eb0-80d6-8c037dcba8d0","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Operational homeostasis rules.
     2→//!
     3→//! These rules monitor error streaks, error rates, and operational
     4→//! health to override operating modes and restrict dangerous actions.
     5→
     6→use autonomic_core::gating::HomeostaticState;
     7→use autonomic_core::rules::{GatingDecision, HomeostaticRule};
     8→
     9→/// Error streak rule: when the agent hits too many consecutive errors,
    10→/// restrict side effects and suggest mode change.
    11→pub struct ErrorStreakRule {
    12→    /// Error rate threshold (errors / total) above which the rule fires.
    13→    pub error_rate_threshold: f64,
    14→    /// Minimum events before the rule can fire (avoid false positives).
    15→    pub min_events: u32,
    16→}
    17→
    18→impl ErrorStreakRule {
    19→    pub fn new(error_rate_threshold: f64, min_events: u32) -> Self {
    20→        Self {
    21→            error_rate_threshold,
    22→            min_events,
    23→        }
    24→    }
    25→}
    26→
    27→impl Default for ErrorStreakRule {
    28→    fn default() -> Self {
    29→        Self::new(0.3, 5) // 30% error rate after at least 5 events
    30→    }
    31→}
    32→
    33→impl HomeostaticRule for ErrorStreakRule {
    34→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
    35→        "error_streak"
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision> {
    39→        let total = state.operational.total_errors + state.operational.total_successes;
    40→        if total < self.min_events {
    41→            return None;
    42→        }
    43→
    44→        let error_rate = state.operational.total_errors as f64 / total as f64;
    45→
    46→        if error_rate > self.error_rate_threshold {
    47→            Some(GatingDecision {
    48→                rule_id: self.rule_id().into(),
    49→                restrict_side_effects: Some(true),
    50→                max_tool_calls_per_tick: Some(3),
    51→                rationale: format!(
    52→                    "error rate {:.0}% ({}/{}) exceeds threshold {:.0}%",
    53→                    error_rate * 100.0,
    54→                    state.operational.total_errors,
    55→                    total,
    56→                    self.error_rate_threshold * 100.0
    57→                ),
    58→                ..GatingDecision::noop(self.rule_id())
    59→            })
    60→        } else {
    61→            None
    62→        }
    63→    }
    64→}
    65→
    66→#[cfg(test)]
    67→mod tests {
    68→    use super::*;
    69→
    70→    #[test]
    71→    fn error_streak_below_min_events() {
    72→        let rule = ErrorStreakRule::default();
    73→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
    74→        state.operational.total_errors = 2;
    75→        state.operational.total_successes = 1;
    76→        // Only 3 total events, min is 5
    77→        assert!(rule.evaluate(&state).is_none());
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    #[test]
    81→    fn error_streak_below_threshold() {
    82→        let rule = ErrorStreakRule::default();
    83→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
    84→        state.operational.total_errors = 1;
    85→        state.operational.total_successes = 9;
    86→        // 10% error rate, threshold is 30%
    87→        assert!(rule.evaluate(&state).is_none());
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    #[test]
    91→    fn error_streak_above_threshold() {
    92→        let rule = ErrorStreakRule::default();
    93→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
    94→        state.operational.total_errors = 4;
    95→        state.operational.total_successes = 6;
    96→        // 40% error rate, threshold is 30%
    97→        let decision = rule.evaluate(&state).unwrap();
    98→        assert_eq!(decision.restrict_side_effects, Some(true));
    99→        assert_eq!(decision.max_tool_calls_per_tick, Some(3));
   100→    }
   101→}
   102→"}]},"uuid":"6faa8094-bd0a-47b0-823b-2e84c2091a36","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:34.431Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"df12f1d4-352c-4eb0-80d6-8c037dcba8d0"}

{"parentUuid":"09a13199-0d1c-4ae1-a8c5-af23ea659701","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QZQV8w9buC1HWt5KVs4EP8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Cognitive homeostasis rules.
     2→//!
     3→//! These rules monitor context pressure and token usage to prevent
     4→//! context overflow and optimize model selection.
     5→
     6→use autonomic_core::ModelTier;
     7→use autonomic_core::gating::HomeostaticState;
     8→use autonomic_core::rules::{GatingDecision, HomeostaticRule};
     9→
    10→/// Context pressure rule: when context is filling up, suggest model downgrade.
    11→///
    12→/// High context pressure means the agent is consuming a large fraction
    13→/// of available tokens. Downgrading to a cheaper/faster model or reducing
    14→/// output length helps avoid hitting context limits.
    15→pub struct ContextPressureRule {
    16→    /// Pressure threshold above which the rule fires (0.0-1.0).
    17→    pub threshold: f32,
    18→}
    19→
    20→impl ContextPressureRule {
    21→    pub fn new(threshold: f32) -> Self {
    22→        Self { threshold }
    23→    }
    24→}
    25→
    26→impl Default for ContextPressureRule {
    27→    fn default() -> Self {
    28→        Self::new(0.8) // 80% context usage
    29→    }
    30→}
    31→
    32→impl HomeostaticRule for ContextPressureRule {
    33→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
    34→        "context_pressure"
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision> {
    38→        if state.cognitive.context_pressure > self.threshold {
    39→            Some(GatingDecision {
    40→                rule_id: self.rule_id().into(),
    41→                preferred_model: Some(ModelTier::Standard),
    42→                max_tokens_next_turn: Some(2048),
    43→                rationale: format!(
    44→                    "context pressure {:.0}% exceeds threshold {:.0}%",
    45→                    state.cognitive.context_pressure * 100.0,
    46→                    self.threshold * 100.0
    47→                ),
    48→                ..GatingDecision::noop(self.rule_id())
    49→            })
    50→        } else {
    51→            None
    52→        }
    53→    }
    54→}
    55→
    56→/// Token exhaustion rule: when tokens remaining are critically low,
    57→/// restrict tool calls to conserve budget.
    58→pub struct TokenExhaustionRule {
    59→    /// Fraction of tokens remaining below which the rule fires (0.0-1.0).
    60→    pub threshold_fraction: f64,
    61→    /// Maximum tool calls allowed when rule fires.
    62→    pub max_tool_calls: u32,
    63→}
    64→
    65→impl TokenExhaustionRule {
    66→    pub fn new(threshold_fraction: f64, max_tool_calls: u32) -> Self {
    67→        Self {
    68→            threshold_fraction,
    69→            max_tool_calls,
    70→        }
    71→    }
    72→}
    73→
    74→impl Default for TokenExhaustionRule {
    75→    fn default() -> Self {
    76→        Self::new(0.1, 2) // 10% remaining → max 2 tool calls
    77→    }
    78→}
    79→
    80→impl HomeostaticRule for TokenExhaustionRule {
    81→    fn rule_id(&self) -> &str {
    82→        "token_exhaustion"
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision> {
    86→        let total = state.cognitive.total_tokens_used + state.cognitive.tokens_remaining;
    87→        if total == 0 {
    88→            return None;
    89→        }
    90→
    91→        let remaining_fraction = state.cognitive.tokens_remaining as f64 / total as f64;
    92→
    93→        if remaining_fraction < self.threshold_fraction {
    94→            Some(GatingDecision {
    95→                rule_id: self.rule_id().into(),
    96→                max_tool_calls_per_tick: Some(self.max_tool_calls),
    97→                max_tokens_next_turn: Some(1024),
    98→                rationale: format!(
    99→                    "tokens {:.0}% remaining — limiting to {} tool calls",
   100→                    remaining_fraction * 100.0,
   101→                    self.max_tool_calls
   102→                ),
   103→                ..GatingDecision::noop(self.rule_id())
   104→            })
   105→        } else {
   106→            None
   107→        }
   108→    }
   109→}
   110→
   111→#[cfg(test)]
   112→mod tests {
   113→    use super::*;
   114→
   115→    #[test]
   116→    fn context_pressure_below_threshold() {
   117→        let rule = ContextPressureRule::default();
   118→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
   119→        state.cognitive.context_pressure = 0.5;
   120→        assert!(rule.evaluate(&state).is_none());
   121→    }
   122→
   123→    #[test]
   124→    fn context_pressure_above_threshold() {
   125→        let rule = ContextPressureRule::default();
   126→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
   127→        state.cognitive.context_pressure = 0.85;
   128→        let decision = rule.evaluate(&state).unwrap();
   129→        assert_eq!(decision.preferred_model, Some(ModelTier::Standard));
   130→        assert_eq!(decision.max_tokens_next_turn, Some(2048));
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    #[test]
   134→    fn token_exhaustion_plenty_remaining() {
   135→        let rule = TokenExhaustionRule::default();
   136→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
   137→        state.cognitive.total_tokens_used = 50_000;
   138→        state.cognitive.tokens_remaining = 70_000;
   139→        assert!(rule.evaluate(&state).is_none());
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    #[test]
   143→    fn token_exhaustion_low_remaining() {
   144→        let rule = TokenExhaustionRule::default();
   145→        let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent("test");
   146→        state.cognitive.total_tokens_used = 110_000;
   147→        state.cognitive.tokens_remaining = 10_000; // ~8.3%
   148→        let decision = rule.evaluate(&state).unwrap();
   149→        assert_eq!(decision.max_tool_calls_per_tick, Some(2));
   150→        assert_eq!(decision.max_tokens_next_turn, Some(1024));
   151→    }
   152→}
   153→"}]},"uuid":"31b3bfe9-695f-4ee9-a7bb-e4fd2c8c202d","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:34.830Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"09a13199-0d1c-4ae1-a8c5-af23ea659701"}

{"parentUuid":"2b5c76ab-7236-4f9e-b97c-f537f3a7514e","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_01RkrrHfnMDWkT6DmeNTfBnN","toolUseID":"toolu_01RkrrHfnMDWkT6DmeNTfBnN","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:37.099Z","uuid":"214447b5-7e1b-4f96-897b-0a65609b67d3"}
{"parentUuid":"38689e50-b296-422e-95f0-74410709dd2d","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RkrrHfnMDWkT6DmeNTfBnN","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Lago journal subscriber.
     2→//!
     3→//! Subscribes to a Lago journal via `Journal.stream()` and feeds events
     4→//! to the projection reducer to maintain per-session `HomeostaticState`.
     5→
     6→use std::collections::HashMap;
     7→use std::sync::Arc;
     8→
     9→use autonomic_controller::fold;
    10→use autonomic_core::gating::HomeostaticState;
    11→use lago_core::journal::Journal;
    12→use tokio::sync::RwLock;
    13→use tokio_stream::StreamExt;
    14→use tracing::{info, warn};
    15→
    16→/// Shared projection state across all sessions.
    17→pub type ProjectionMap = Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HomeostaticState>>>;
    18→
    19→/// Create a new empty projection map.
    20→pub fn new_projection_map() -> ProjectionMap {
    21→    Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new()))
    22→}
    23→
    24→/// Subscribe to a Lago journal for a specific session and continuously
    25→/// update the projection map.
    26→///
    27→/// This function runs until the stream ends or an error occurs.
    28→/// It should be spawned as a tokio task.
    29→pub async fn subscribe_session(
    30→    journal: Arc<dyn Journal>,
    31→    session_id: String,
    32→    branch_id: String,
    33→    projections: ProjectionMap,
    34→) {
    35→    let lago_session_id = lago_core::id::SessionId::from_string(&session_id);
    36→    let lago_branch_id = lago_core::id::BranchId::from_string(&branch_id);
    37→
    38→    // Get starting sequence from existing projection
    39→    let after_seq = {
    40→        let map = projections.read().await;
    41→        map.get(&session_id).map_or(0, |s| s.last_event_seq)
    42→    };
    43→
    44→    info!(
    45→        session_id = %session_id,
    46→        branch_id = %branch_id,
    47→        after_seq = after_seq,
    48→        "subscribing to Lago journal"
    49→    );
    50→
    51→    let stream_result = journal
    52→        .stream(lago_session_id, lago_branch_id, after_seq)
    53→        .await;
    54→
    55→    let mut stream = match stream_result {
    56→        Ok(s) => s,
    57→        Err(e) => {
    58→            warn!(
    59→                session_id = %session_id,
    60→                error = %e,
    61→                "failed to subscribe to Lago journal"
    62→            );
    63→            return;
    64→        }
    65→    };
    66→
    67→    while let Some(result) = stream.next().await {
    68→        match result {
    69→            Ok(envelope) => {
    70→                let seq = envelope.seq;
    71→                let ts_ms = envelope.timestamp / 1000; // microseconds → milliseconds
    72→
    73→                let mut map = projections.write().await;
    74→                let state = map
    75→                    .entry(session_id.clone())
    76→                    .or_insert_with(|| HomeostaticState::for_agent(&session_id));
    77→                *state = fold(state.clone(), &envelope.payload, seq, ts_ms);
    78→            }
    79→            Err(e) => {
    80→                warn!(
    81→                    session_id = %session_id,
    82→                    error = %e,
    83→                    "error reading from Lago journal stream"
    84→                );
    85→            }
    86→        }
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    info!(session_id = %session_id, "Lago journal stream ended");
    90→}
    91→
    92→/// Load initial projection state by reading all existing events for a session.
    93→pub async fn load_projection(
    94→    journal: Arc<dyn Journal>,
    95→    session_id: &str,
    96→    branch_id: &str,
    97→) -> Result<HomeostaticState, lago_core::error::LagoError> {
    98→    let query = lago_core::journal::EventQuery::new()
    99→        .session(lago_core::id::SessionId::from_string(session_id))
   100→        .branch(lago_core::id::BranchId::from_string(branch_id));
   101→
   102→    let events = journal.read(query).await?;
   103→
   104→    let mut state = HomeostaticState::for_agent(session_id);
   105→    for envelope in &events {
   106→        let ts_ms = envelope.timestamp / 1000;
   107→        state = fold(state, &envelope.payload, envelope.seq, ts_ms);
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    Ok(state)
   111→}
   112→"}]},"uuid":"44087ec3-61ab-4b1c-8721-151e6b62cf7e","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:37.098Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"38689e50-b296-422e-95f0-74410709dd2d"}

{"parentUuid":"b187ba46-6b8c-4892-ab19-eec47a1af7ac","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Event publisher: writes Autonomic decisions back to Lago.
     2→//!
     3→//! This allows the Autonomic controller's decisions to be persisted
     4→//! in the event journal, enabling replay and audit.
     5→
     6→use std::sync::Arc;
     7→
     8→use autonomic_core::events::AutonomicEvent;
     9→use lago_core::event::EventEnvelope;
    10→use lago_core::id::{BranchId, EventId, SeqNo, SessionId};
    11→use lago_core::journal::Journal;
    12→use tracing::warn;
    13→
    14→/// Publish an Autonomic event to the Lago journal.
    15→pub async fn publish_event(
    16→    journal: Arc<dyn Journal>,
    17→    session_id: &str,
    18→    branch_id: &str,
    19→    event: AutonomicEvent,
    20→) -> Result<SeqNo, lago_core::error::LagoError> {
    21→    let envelope = EventEnvelope {
    22→        event_id: EventId::new(),
    23→        session_id: SessionId::from_string(session_id),
    24→        branch_id: BranchId::from_string(branch_id),
    25→        run_id: None,
    26→        seq: 0, // Journal assigns the actual sequence number
    27→        timestamp: lago_core::event::EventEnvelope::now_micros(),
    28→        parent_id: None,
    29→        payload: event.into_event_kind(),
    30→        metadata: std::collections::HashMap::new(),
    31→        schema_version: 1,
    32→    };
    33→
    34→    journal.append(envelope).await
    35→}
    36→
    37→/// Publish a batch of Autonomic events atomically.
    38→pub async fn publish_events(
    39→    journal: Arc<dyn Journal>,
    40→    session_id: &str,
    41→    branch_id: &str,
    42→    events: Vec<AutonomicEvent>,
    43→) -> Result<SeqNo, lago_core::error::LagoError> {
    44→    if events.is_empty() {
    45→        warn!("publish_events called with empty event list");
    46→        // Return 0 as a no-op sequence number
    47→        return Ok(0);
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    let envelopes: Vec<EventEnvelope> = events
    51→        .into_iter()
    52→        .map(|event| EventEnvelope {
    53→            event_id: EventId::new(),
    54→            session_id: SessionId::from_string(session_id),
    55→            branch_id: BranchId::from_string(branch_id),
    56→            run_id: None,
    57→            seq: 0,
    58→            timestamp: lago_core::event::EventEnvelope::now_micros(),
    59→            parent_id: None,
    60→            payload: event.into_event_kind(),
    61→            metadata: std::collections::HashMap::new(),
    62→            schema_version: 1,
    63→        })
    64→        .collect();
    65→
    66→    journal.append_batch(envelopes).await
    67→}
    68→"}]},"uuid":"8814e130-97dd-4070-b7ee-3d9f4bec0761","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:38.687Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b187ba46-6b8c-4892-ab19-eec47a1af7ac"}

{"parentUuid":"b346a42f-ee62-4bdb-86a8-6671c2468f30","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life","sessionId":"60589102-adb0-4b3d-856a-c464c7cc580a","version":"2.1.42","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"ad5ed02","slug":"quirky-leaping-aho","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019a4Jvy5zBnDt9oWBWAnDk3","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→//! Shared application state for the HTTP API.
     2→
     3→use std::collections::HashMap;
     4→use std::sync::Arc;
     5→
     6→use autonomic_core::gating::HomeostaticState;
     7→use autonomic_core::rules::RuleSet;
     8→use tokio::sync::RwLock;
     9→
    10→/// Shared state for the axum HTTP server.
    11→#[derive(Clone)]
    12→pub struct AppState {
    13→    /// Per-session homeostatic projections.
    14→    pub projections: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HomeostaticState>>>,
    15→    /// The rule set used for evaluation.
    16→    pub rules: Arc<RuleSet>,
    17→}
    18→
    19→impl AppState {
    20→    /// Create a new application state with the given rule set.
    21→    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
    22→        Self {
    23→            projections: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),
    24→            rules: Arc::new(rules),
    25→        }
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    /// Create an application state with a pre-populated projection map.
    29→    pub fn with_projections(
    30→        projections: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HomeostaticState>>>,
    31→        rules: RuleSet,
    32→    ) -> Self {
    33→        Self {
    34→            projections,
    35→            rules: Arc::new(rules),
    36→        }
    37→    }
    38→}
    39→"}]},"uuid":"9d1c3e0e-a7fb-4a74-9aeb-271daf6a97fd","timestamp":"2026-03-03T22:05:41.291Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b346a42f-ee62-4bdb-86a8-6671c2468f30"}
